title: Platform Site Promotion + Rollback (dev → staging → prod with release tags)
label: site-promotion
purpose: Formalize the promotion ladder for FAMtastic sites so dev/staging/main branches map cleanly to local/Netlify-staging/Netlify-prod, and so any production deploy can be rolled back to a prior release tag in one command.
why_now: MBSH canonicalization on 2026-05-05 surfaced that the promote/rollback flow exists in convention only — branches exist on every site but no command formalizes the transitions, no release tags are written, and no rollback path is wired. Without this, a bad prod deploy has no clean revert.

origin.summary: User asked to confirm site repos are isolated from Studio history, and to design promote-to-prod + rollback procedures with release tags or branches.

success_signal: Running `platform site promote mbsh-reunion --to prod` performs: merge staging→main, write release tag (date-based), push, wait for Netlify prod deploy, write proof. `platform site rollback mbsh-reunion` reverts main to the previous tag. Both work end-to-end against MBSH and at least one other site.
non_goals: Replacing Netlify's deploy mechanism (Netlify still does the actual deploy on push).
Automating DNS changes for new domains (handled by separate platform/dns/* capabilities).

workstreams.title: platform site promote <tag> --to <staging|prod>
outputs: platform/capabilities/site/promote.sh
Merges current branch → target branch (dev→staging or staging→main)
On --to prod: writes annotated release tag using date-based scheme (vYYYY.MM.DD-HHMM)
Pushes branch + tag to origin
Polls Netlify deploy status until success/failure
Writes proof to proofs/site-promote-<tag>-<env>-<date>.json
Refuses promotion if working tree is dirty or tests are failing (configurable)
acceptance: platform site promote mbsh-reunion --to staging merges dev→staging, pushes, and waits for staging Netlify deploy.
platform site promote mbsh-reunion --to prod merges staging→main, writes tag, pushes, waits for prod Netlify deploy.
Proof file records: source branch, target branch, merge commit SHA, tag, Netlify deploy URL, deploy status.

workstreams.title: platform site rollback <tag> [--to <tag-name>]
outputs: platform/capabilities/site/rollback.sh
Default behavior: revert main to the previous release tag
--to <tag-name>: revert main to a specific named tag
Implementation: git checkout <tag> -- frontend/ backend/ config/ → commit on main with revert message → push
Writes proof to proofs/site-rollback-<tag>-<date>.json with from/to tag SHAs
acceptance: Rollback reverts site to a named or implicit prior tag.
Rollback never deletes git history; it always creates a new revert commit.
Netlify auto-redeploys on the new commit and serves the prior version.

workstreams.title: Release tag scheme — date-based for sites
outputs: Tag format: v<YYYY>.<MM>.<DD>-<HHMM> (UTC)
Tag message captures: site tag, branch, commit SHA, deployer (vault user), Netlify deploy id
Documented in docs/operating-rules/site-release-tags.md
acceptance: Every prod deploy writes exactly one tag.
Tags are pushable, listable, and addressable by rollback.

workstreams.title: Two Netlify projects per site: <tag>-staging and <tag>
outputs: Convention: every site has TWO Netlify projects: <tag>-staging (production_branch=staging) and <tag> (production_branch=main, custom domain)
platform/capabilities/deploy/connect-netlify.sh updated to create/configure both
Documented in docs/operating-rules/site-netlify-convention.md
acceptance: Running connect-netlify creates both projects and links each to the right branch.
Existing sites can be retroactively brought into compliance via a --reconcile flag.

workstreams.title: Promote/rollback proof capture (Ops integration)
outputs: Each promote/rollback writes a proof packet to proofs/ with type='site-promotion' or 'site-rollback'
Cross-link to plan and to the Ops Reviews tab (when Ops workspace ships)

workstreams.title: Governance gates on prod promotion
outputs: Promotion to prod requires governance approval token matching plans/registry.json governance.hard_stop_conditions for production_deploy
Rollback bypasses the gate when --reason 'incident' is supplied (faster recovery), but writes a louder proof
acceptance: Prod promote without token is refused.
Rollback with --reason incident proceeds and writes proof flagged 'emergency'.

scope: ws_promote_command working for staging and prod
ws_rollback_command working for default-prev-tag and named-tag
Manual one-time creation of the prod Netlify project for MBSH (Netlify git-link still requires UI)

known_gaps_opened: Netlify ↔ GitHub linking still requires manual UI step (Netlify API limitation, documented in connect-netlify.sh).
No prod Netlify project exists for MBSH yet — must be created (manually) before first prod promote.
platform/capabilities/site/promote.sh and rollback.sh do not exist yet.
